questions.q: In which city is the Parliament House of India located?
questions.explanation: The Parliament House of India is located in New Delhi.

questions.q: In which year was the original Parliament House inaugurated?
questions.explanation: The original Parliament House was inaugurated in 1927.

questions.q: Which two architects are credited with designing the original Parliament House?
options: Robert Tor Russell and Walter George
questions.explanation: The original Parliament House was designed by Edwin Lutyens and Herbert Baker.

questions.q: Parliament House serves as the seat of which institution?
questions.explanation: Parliament House is the seat of India's legislature.

questions.q: A new Parliament building was inaugurated in which year?
questions.explanation: A new Parliament building was inaugurated in 2023.

questions.q: Which two Houses of Parliament meet in the Parliament complex?
options: The High Court and the Supreme Court
The Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha
The Vidhan Sabha and the Vidhan Parishad
questions.explanation: The Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha are the two Houses that meet in the Parliament complex.

questions.q: The original Parliament House is well known for what distinctive shape?
questions.explanation: The original Parliament House is famous for its circular shape.

questions.q: Parliament House in New Delhi was built during which period of Indian history?
questions.explanation: The original Parliament House was built during the British colonial period and completed in 1927.

questions.q: Parliament House is part of the central administrative area of New Delhi often associated with which architect's planning?
questions.explanation: Parliament House lies in the Lutyens-planned central area of New Delhi.

questions.q: What is one main activity conducted inside Parliament House?
questions.explanation: Parliament House is where laws are debated and made for the country.

questions.q: The original Parliament House was first known by which name when it opened?
questions.explanation: When it opened in 1927, the building was known as the Council House.

questions.q: Who laid the foundation stone of the original Parliament House in 1921?
questions.explanation: The foundation stone of the original Parliament House was laid in February 1921 by HRH the Duke of Connaught.

questions.q: The famous Central Hall of the old Parliament House is historically significant as the venue where what occurred?
options: The Constitution of India was framed and adopted
The first Olympic Games of India were held
The first films of India were screened
questions.explanation: The Central Hall hosted the Constituent Assembly, where the Constitution of India was framed and adopted.

questions.q: The construction of the original Parliament House took approximately how long, being completed in 1927?
questions.explanation: Construction began in 1921 and was completed in 1927, taking about six years.

questions.q: Within the old Parliament House, the two main legislative chambers were arranged around which feature?
options: A central circular hall and garden courts
questions.explanation: The circular building arranged its chambers around the Central Hall and garden courts.

questions.q: Edwin Lutyens, co-designer of Parliament House, is also strongly associated with designing which other landmark in New Delhi?
questions.explanation: Edwin Lutyens is also associated with the design of Rashtrapati Bhavan in New Delhi.

questions.q: The old Parliament House is widely recognised for the ring of tall columns around its exterior, numbering how many?
questions.explanation: The circular colonnade of the old Parliament House features around 144 columns.

questions.q: The new Parliament building inaugurated in 2023 was constructed as part of which redevelopment initiative?
questions.explanation: The new Parliament building was constructed as part of the Central Vista redevelopment project.

questions.q: What is the broad shape of the new Parliament building inaugurated in 2023?
questions.explanation: The new Parliament building has a triangular shape, contrasting with the circular old building.

questions.q: During the colonial era, the legislative body that met in the Council House included which chamber?
questions.explanation: The colonial-era Central Legislative Assembly met in the Council House before independence.

questions.q: The historic 'Tryst with Destiny' address on the eve of independence was delivered in which hall of Parliament House?
questions.explanation: The 'Tryst with Destiny' speech was delivered in the Central Hall of Parliament House.

questions.q: Herbert Baker, who co-designed Parliament House, also worked with Lutyens on the broader design of which complex?
options: The Secretariat Buildings on Raisina Hill
questions.explanation: Herbert Baker designed the Secretariat Buildings on Raisina Hill alongside Lutyens's wider New Delhi plan.

questions.q: The old Parliament House complex includes a domed Central Hall whose diameter is approximately what?
questions.explanation: The Central Hall is a large circular chamber roughly 30 metres in diameter under its dome.

questions.q: Before being called Parliament House after independence, the building primarily housed which colonial legislative chambers?
options: The Army Headquarters and the Mint
The Privy Council and the High Court
The Board of Revenue and the Treasury
The Central Legislative Assembly, the Council of State, and the Chamber of Princes
questions.explanation: The Council House held the Central Legislative Assembly, the Council of State, and the Chamber of Princes.

questions.q: In what architectural style is the old Parliament House broadly described, blending classical forms with Indian motifs?
questions.explanation: The old Parliament House blends classical European forms with Indian decorative motifs.

questions.q: The new Parliament building has a notably larger seating capacity in the Lok Sabha Chamber, designed to accommodate roughly how many members?
questions.explanation: The new Parliament building's Lok Sabha Chamber is designed to seat around 888 members.

questions.q: In the new Parliament building, a historic sceptre known by which name was installed near the Speaker's chair?
questions.explanation: A historic sceptre called the Sengol was installed near the Speaker's chair in the new Parliament building.

questions.q: The old Parliament House is located close to which other circular feature of central New Delhi's road plan?
questions.explanation: The old Parliament House lies near Connaught Place, another circular feature of Lutyens's Delhi.

questions.q: What material predominantly gives the old Parliament House its characteristic colour, like much of Lutyens's Delhi?
questions.explanation: Red and cream sandstone gives the old Parliament House and much of central New Delhi their characteristic look.

questions.q: After the new Parliament building opened, the old Parliament House was envisaged to be preserved and repurposed for what kind of use?
questions.explanation: Following the opening of the new building, the old Parliament House was envisaged for heritage and museum-related use.
